assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'All 4 slices will be recreated simultaneously — not rolling' and 'Atomic Recreation Behavior'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Cascading restart: A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: States 'Your entire 1,024-chip training job will be forcibly terminated' and 'Catastrophic training interruption is inevitable'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Mentions 'potentially longer if TPU capacity is constrained', 'potentially much longer if TPU resources are unavailable', and discusses 'TPU capacity exhaustion' scenarios

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Checkpoint Immediately (Critical)' and 'Force checkpoint now, don't wait for scheduled interval'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Recommends 'Test checkpoint restoration' and 'verify your job can resume from checkpoints without manual intervention'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command for maintenance exclusion and states 'Apply 30-day maintenance exclusion only if training completes soon'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance exclusions to defer events, it does not discuss scheduling maintenance windows during planned breaks in training
